scenario: Quantum Healthcare uses Camunda 8.8 to triage 4000 records per day. User tasks must render a structured form inside Tasklist (not in an external web app) and the same form must also be reusable in a public start-event form for self-service customers.
question: Which form technology rendered inside Tasklist matches this requirement?

options.text: Camunda Forms; one form definition is rendered by Tasklist for user tasks and by the public start-event form runtime for self-service start
An external React app embedded into Tasklist via iframe; this is the documented way to render structured forms inside Tasklist user tasks
BPMN forms; the BPMN spec includes a form definition language that Tasklist renders natively for both user tasks and start events
OpenAPI-driven forms; Tasklist generates the form from the connected job worker's published OpenAPI definition for the user task

a.text: Correct. Camunda Forms is the supported form technology in Camunda 8; one form definition is rendered by Tasklist and the public form runtime.

b.text: Incorrect. iframe-embedded React apps are not the documented form mechanism; Camunda Forms is the supported in-product form system.

c.text: Incorrect. The BPMN spec does not define a form language; Camunda Forms is a separate Camunda-specific format.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no OpenAPI-driven Tasklist form generation; forms are authored in the Camunda Forms file format.

explanation: Camunda Forms is the documented form technology in 8.8; the same form definition renders inside Tasklist for user tasks and in the public form runtime for start events.
